section.title: Overview
section.para: The rust_dynamic_blacklist module provides a live-reload file-based blacklist for blocking &sip; traffic by source &ip;, User-Agent, domain, or any arbitrary string value. The blacklist file is loaded at startup into each worker process. Matching can be exact (HashSet lookup) or prefix-based (linear scan of prefixes).
This module is useful for blocking known scanner &ip; addresses, malicious User-Agent strings, or spam domains without restarting &osips;.

section.title: How it works
section.para: At worker initialization, each process loads the blacklist file and parses it line-by-line. Blank lines and lines starting with # are skipped.
In exact mode, entries are stored in a HashSet for O(1) lookup. The value passed to rust_check_blacklist() must match an entry exactly (case-sensitive).

In prefix mode, entries are stored as a list of prefixes. The value is checked against each prefix using starts_with(). This allows blocking entire &ip; subnets (e.g., 192.168.1.) or User-Agent families (e.g., friendly-scanner).
Matching is case-sensitive in both modes.

The blacklist can be reloaded at runtime using rust_blacklist_reload() without restarting &osips;. The reload atomically swaps the entire data set so that in-flight lookups see either the old or the new data, never a partial update.

section.title: &osips; Modules
section.para:
The following modules must be loaded before this module:
None.

section.title: External Libraries or Applications
section.para:
The following libraries or applications must be installed before running &osips; with this module loaded:
None.

section.title: blacklist_file (string)
section.para: Path to the blacklist file. One entry per line. Lines starting with # are treated as comments. Blank lines are ignored. This parameter is required. The module will fail to load if this parameter is not set or the file does not exist.

para.emphasis: Default value is None (must be set).

example.title: Blacklist file format
example.programlisting: # /etc/opensips/blacklist.txt # IP addresses 192.168.1.100 10.0.0. # User-Agent strings friendly-scanner SIPVicious # Domains sip.spam.example.com

section.title: match_mode (string)
section.para:
The matching algorithm to use:
exact - value must match a blacklist entry exactly (case-sensitive). Uses a HashSet for O(1) lookups.
prefix - value must start with a blacklist entry (case-sensitive). Uses linear prefix matching.
Check whether the given value matches any entry in the blacklist. The matching behavior depends on the match_mode parameter.
The return value convention is positive = allowed so that typical usage reads naturally as an if-condition.
Meaning of the parameters is as follows:

listitem.para: value (string) - The value to check against the blacklist. Typically a pseudo-variable such as $si (source &ip;), $ua (User-Agent), or $rd (domain). This parameter is mandatory.

section.para: Return codes:

listitem.para: 1 - value is NOT in the blacklist (allowed).
-2 (error) - internal error (missing parameter or uninitialized worker).

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: rust_check_blacklist usage

section.para: Re-read the blacklist file from disk and atomically swap the in-memory data. This allows updating the blacklist without restarting &osips;. The reload affects only the worker process that executes the function. To reload all workers, trigger this function from a route that is processed by each worker (e.g., via an MI-triggered OPTIONS ping or timer route).
On success, the entries and reloads statistics are updated.
Return codes:

listitem.para: -2 (error) - file read error or worker not initialized.

section.para: This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

example.title: rust_blacklist_reload usage

section.para: Retrieve module statistics as a JSON string. Sets $var(blacklist_stats) with a JSON object containing all tracked counters for the current worker process.
Return codes:
This function can be used from REQUEST_ROUTE, FAILURE_ROUTE, and ONREPLY_ROUTE.

section.title: $var(blacklist_stats)
section.para: JSON string containing the module statistics for the current worker process. Set by rust_blacklist_stats().
Total number of blacklist lookups performed.
Number of lookups that matched the blacklist (traffic blocked).
Number of lookups that did not match the blacklist (traffic allowed).
Number of entries currently loaded from the blacklist file. Updated on reload.
Number of successful hot reloads performed via rust_blacklist_reload().
